leagueBriefing: Borussia Mönchengladbach snapped a three-match winless streak with a professional 2-0 victory over a struggling St. Pauli side at BORUSSIA-PARK. A spectacular free-kick from Kevin Stöger provided the breakthrough in the first half, and Gladbach never looked back, controlling the tempo and adding a second after the break to secure all three points and move further away from the danger zone.

games.headline: Stöger free-kick spark as Gladbach cruises past St. Pauli
games.brief: Borussia Mönchengladbach delivered a dominant performance on Friday night, effectively neutralizing St. Pauli's attack to claim a 2-0 win. Kevin Stöger stole the show in the 37th minute with a clinical direct free-kick that left the keeper rooted to the spot. While St. Pauli enjoyed significant possession (60%), they lacked the cutting edge to trouble Moritz Nicolas. Franck Honorat sealed the result in the 62nd minute, latching onto a Joseph Scally assist to ensure Gladbach avenged their earlier DFB-Pokal exit against the same opponents.
bullets: Kevin Stöger opened the scoring with a brilliant direct free-kick in the 37th minute.
Franck Honorat doubled the lead in the 62nd minute, his 6th goal contribution of the season.
St. Pauli dominated possession with 60% but managed only one shot on target throughout the 90 minutes.
The victory marks Gladbach's first clean sheet in five matches, moving them up to 12th in the table.
Attendance at BORUSSIA-PARK was recorded at 49,202 fans.
games.context: The win provides Gladbach with breathing room in the mid-table, while St. Pauli remains precariously close to the relegation playoff spot.
